design_note: MaxPriorityQueue wraps heapq (Python's min-heap). Bug is multi-site: push() must negate priority for max-heap behavior AND peek() must re-negate the stored priority before returning it to the caller. A naive fix that only negates at push leaks the internal -priority through peek's return value (catches caller). A naive fix that only changes pop semantics (e.g. nlargest) breaks tie-break ordering (FIFO required). The correct fix coordinates push() + peek() and preserves a counter-based stable ordering for ties.
